content:
Case 3:
Initial presentation:
- Age: 71
- Sex: male
- Chief complaint: Fatigue, generalized weakness, exertional dyspnea, and persistent transfusion dependency
- Vital signs: BP 128/70 mmHg, HR 105/min, respiratory rate 22/min, T° 37.4 °C, oxygen saturation 95% on room air.
Setting: Outpatient/Day Unit (Initial Presentation)
Capabilities: You are evaluating an older patient with new or worsening symptoms in an ambulatory or day-unit setting. You can obtain a focused history and examination, request baseline laboratory evaluations, and arrange standard non-invasive imaging appropriate for this level of care. Invasive procedures and complex diagnostics usually require additional coordination and are not immediately available at the point of first contact.
Provide either a request for a single information item or solve.

response.request: focused history (timeline of cytopenias/transfusion needs; bleeding/infections; constitutional symptoms; medication/toxin exposure; prior hematologic diagnosis or chemotherapy/radiation)

content:
History Physical:
On August 5, 2024, the patient presented with fatigue, generalized weakness, and inability to exercise, accompanied by exertional dyspnea. Clinical signs of sarcopenia were present. He reported urinary incontinence following prior prostate cancer surgery (Gleason 7b). He had pneumonia three weeks earlier, which was treated with intravenous antibiotics at another hospital. Persistent transfusion-dependency (1/week).
Physical examination: Pale conjunctivae and reduced muscle mass consistent with sarcopenia. Mild exertional dyspnea observed on ambulation. No lymphadenopathy or hepatosplenomegaly. Cardiopulmonary examination otherwise unremarkable. Surgical scar in the pelvic region; mild urinary incontinence reported. No peripheral edema. Stable weight over the past month.
Current medication: Metformin 1,000 mg 1-0-1, empagliflozin 10 mg 1-0-0, ramipril 5 mg 1-0-0, amlodipine 5 mg 1-0-0, bisoprolol 5 mg 1-0-1, apixaban 2.5 mg 1-0-0, atorvastatin 20 mg 1-0-0.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "reticulocyte count (and reticulocyte index)"}}

'reticulocyte count (and reticulocyte index)' not available. Try 'cbc' instead.
This case does not list a separate reticulocyte count item. A complete blood count (CBC) is available in this round and may include red cell indices relevant to anemia workup; consider requesting the CBC.
(4 invalid requests remaining before case is cancelled. Consider solving with available information or requesting a different item.)

platelet transfusion support per thresholds (and urgently if any bleeding/procedures); stop/hold apixaban given Plt 18 \u00d710\u2079/L unless compelling indication and hematology/cardiology agreement", "intention": "supportive", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Neutropenia management: strict fever precautions and same-day evaluation for any fever/infectious symptoms; solve.working_diagnosis: Bone marrow failure syndrome with severe pancytopenia—most suspicious for high-risk myelodysplastic syndrome (MDS) versus evolving/aleukemic AML
differentials: Bone marrow infiltration/replacement (e.g., metastatic prostate cancer, lymphoma, myelofibrosis)
Severe nutritional deficiency (B12/folate) or less likely hemolysis/consumption

treatment_plan.action: Arrange urgent bone marrow aspirate/biopsy with flow cytometry, cytogenetics/FISH, and myeloid NGS to distinguish MDS vs AML vs aplasia/infiltration

Immediate supportive care for cytopenias: RBC transfusions for symptomatic anemia; platelet transfusion support per thresholds (and urgently if any bleeding/procedures); stop/hold apixaban given Plt 18 ×10⁹/L unless compelling indication and hematology/cardiology agreement

Neutropenia management: strict fever precautions and same-day evaluation for any fever/infectious symptoms; consider antibacterial prophylaxis per institutional policy if prolonged severe neutropenia is anticipated; ensure vaccinations as appropriate when stable
Targeted secondary workup while awaiting marrow: B12/folate, TSH, hemolysis labs (haptoglobin, DAT, retic if available), hepatitis/HIV as indicated; medication and toxin/alcohol exposure review

After marrow confirmation: if higher-risk MDS or AML unfit for intensive induction, plan hypomethylating agent-based therapy (e.g., azacitidine/decitabine; add venetoclax if AML) and assess for clinical trial eligibility; if fit and AML, discuss intensive induction options
Supportive optimization: address sarcopenia (nutrition/physical therapy), manage renal/hepatic dysfunction and correct electrolytes (e.g., hypokalemia), and coordinate close transfusion follow-up

response.request: bone marrow aspirate and biopsy report (morphology: cellularity, lineage dysplasia, blast percentage, fibrosis)

content:
Bone Marrow:
Bone marrow (July 28, 2024): Morphology revealed hypocellular bone marrow with 25–30% myeloblasts. Dysplastic changes were noted across myeloid lineages, characterized by toxic granulation and hypolobulated nuclei. Erythropoiesis and granulopoiesis were markedly reduced; megakaryocytes were decreased and dysplastic.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "bone marrow flow cytometry / immunophenotyping results"}}

response.request: bone marrow flow cytometry / immunophenotyping results

content:
Bone Marrow Flow:
Flow cytometry: Blasts (26% of total nucleated cells) expressed CD34, CD117, HLA-DR, CD13, CD33, and CD38. Partial expression of CD15 and weak MPO positivity were observed. Aberrant co-expression of CD5 and CD7 was noted. Negative for lymphoid markers (CD19, CD20, CD10, CD79a, CD3, TdT). Maturing myeloid and monocytic populations were markedly reduced, with preserved but quantitatively decreased erythroid precursors.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "cytogenetics/FISH (karyotype) results from bone marrow"}}

Cytogenetics:
Preliminary cytogenetics: 46,XY,+1,der(1;16)(q10;p10),t(1;13)(p22;q13),t(9;17)(q22;q12).

TP53/ASXL1/RUNX1), FLT3-ITD/TKD, NPM1, CEBPA, IDH1/2 (and finalize karyotype), plus baseline coagulation/fibrinogen", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Initiate AML-directed therapy appropriate for fitness: if not candidate for intensive induction, start azacitidine (or decitabine) plus venetoclax with inpatient monitoring for cytopenias and infections; if deemed fit, discuss intensive induction vs trial", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Cytopenia and bleeding management: RBC transfusions for symptoms/thresholds; platelet transfusions with strict bleeding precautions; hold apixaban at platelets 18\u00d710\u2079/L unless compelling indication and specialist agreement", "intention": "supportive", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Infection prevention/management for ANC 0.6: immediate febrile neutropenia pathway for any fever; institute antimicrobial prophylaxis per local protocol during expected prolonged neutropenia (commonly antibacterial + mold-active antifungal, with drug\u2013drug interaction management if on venetoclax)", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Tumor lysis prevention and monitoring when starting therapy: IV/oral hydration as appropriate, allopurinol (or rasburicase if high risk), and frequent labs (K/Phos/Ca/uric acid/Cr/LDH)", "intention": "supportive", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Early goals-of-care and consolidation planning: adverse-risk discussion; HLA typing and transplant consultation to assess reduced-intensity allogeneic HSCT candidacy if remission achieved", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 3}]}}}

solve.working_diagnosis: Acute myeloid leukemia (AML), most consistent with AML with myelodysplasia-related features (AML-MR) given multilineage dysplasia and complex cytogenetics, presenting as hypocellular AML with profound pancytopenia
differentials: Myelodysplastic neoplasm with increased blasts evolving to AML (historical distinction; currently blasts >20% supports AML)
Therapy-related myeloid neoplasm (if prior pelvic radiotherapy/chemotherapy exposure is elicited)
Hypocellular MDS with excess blasts (less likely given 25–30% blasts)

treatment_plan.action: Complete AML genetic workup for risk/targeted therapy selection: rapid myeloid NGS (incl. TP53/ASXL1/RUNX1), FLT3-ITD/TKD, NPM1, CEBPA, IDH1/2 (and finalize karyotype), plus baseline coagulation/fibrinogen

Initiate AML-directed therapy appropriate for fitness: if not candidate for intensive induction, start azacitidine (or decitabine) plus venetoclax with inpatient monitoring for cytopenias and infections; if deemed fit, discuss intensive induction vs trial

Cytopenia and bleeding management: RBC transfusions for symptoms/thresholds; platelet transfusions with strict bleeding precautions; hold apixaban at platelets 18×10⁹/L unless compelling indication and specialist agreement

Infection prevention/management for ANC 0.6: immediate febrile neutropenia pathway for any fever; institute antimicrobial prophylaxis per local protocol during expected prolonged neutropenia (commonly antibacterial + mold-active antifungal, with drug–drug interaction management if on venetoclax)
Tumor lysis prevention and monitoring when starting therapy: IV/oral hydration as appropriate, allopurinol (or rasburicase if high risk), and frequent labs (K/Phos/Ca/uric acid/Cr/LDH)
Early goals-of-care and consolidation planning: adverse-risk discussion; HLA typing and transplant consultation to assess reduced-intensity allogeneic HSCT candidacy if remission achieved

Request additional information or solve.
Final round - provide definitive diagnosis and treatment plan.

content:
Molecular Panel:
Mutations in DDX41 (VAF 43%), ASXL1 (VAF 27%), RUNX1 (VAF 34%), TET2 (VAF 41%), and PTPN11 (VAF 11%). No mutations in NPM1, FLT3-ITD/TKD, IDH1/2, CEBPA, or TP53. No KMT2A rearrangement detected.

solve.working_diagnosis: Acute myeloid leukemia with myelodysplasia-related features (AML-MR) / AML with myelodysplasia-related gene mutations (ASXL1, RUNX1) and complex karyotype; concurrent DDX41 mutation at ~50% VAF suspicious for germline myeloid predisposition
differentials: Therapy-related myeloid neoplasm (if prior prostate cancer radiotherapy/chemotherapy exposure is confirmed)
Hypocellular MDS/AML overlap (historic label; blasts 25–30% supports AML)
Aplastic anemia with clonal evolution (less likely given 25–30% immunophenotypic blasts)

treatment_plan.action: Confirm disease classification and prognostic assignment (WHO/ICC/ELN 2022): AML-MR (ASXL1/RUNX1; complex karyotype) and document adverse-risk status; ensure full cytogenetic report is finalized
Order DDX41 germline evaluation (non-hematopoietic tissue such as skin fibroblast/saliva per local practice) with genetic counseling; do NOT use related donors until germline status is clarified

Start AML therapy with curative intent if patient is transplant-eligible/fit: CPX-351 induction (preferred regimen for AML-MR in older adults when intensive therapy is appropriate), with response assessment and re-induction/consolidation per protocol

Supportive care during induction: transfuse RBCs/platelets to institutional thresholds, strict bleeding precautions, and hold apixaban given platelets ~18×10^9/L unless a compelling indication is agreed with cardiology/hematology

Infection and complications prevention while profoundly cytopenic: febrile neutropenia pathway for any fever; antimicrobial prophylaxis per local protocol (e.g., antibacterial + mold-active antifungal + antiviral as indicated) and adjust for interactions/organ function

Allogeneic HSCT plan: initiate unrelated donor search/HLA typing now; if CR achieved and comorbidity/geriatric assessment supports it, proceed to reduced-intensity allogeneic HSCT in CR1 (given adverse-risk AML-MR)

If deemed unfit for intensive induction or declines it: treat with azacitidine (or decitabine) + venetoclax as lower-intensity therapy, with transplant reconsideration if remission and functional improvement occur
